reviews.review_text:
Kovaak is more than just aim training to me.
It is a spiritual comfort, a meditation. It's both a punishment and a redemption to me.
A pure land that belongs only to me.

ngl the ui needs some work and it a bit overwhelming in the beginning knowing where to start, the best and most popular playlists being found on evxl.app and cant be found in game ready to play is shame
it sometimes freezes and stuff so thats also annoying

 but anyways very fun way to train your aim imo
Viscose's benchmarks (KovaaKsScreamingPulledEgg -easier benchmark code great way to start) is amazing for overall improvement divided into muscle groups and aim types and a fun way to improve while chasing scores/getting to the next rank in the benchmarks^^ 

i just love tracking sausages and clicking balls what can i say

Genuinely useful tool and well worth the money if you want to improve your muscle memory with aiming. Just make sure you understand why and why not something works and be consistent with it, and you'll absolutely see progress with your aim in other games.
this game is great altho i have to play it on 40-60% resolution scale to run playable fps i notice around ranges from 1%-40% have the same fps and 40%-60% same fps
Great to get better mouse control, I can totally see the difference in my aim after only 3 weeks of use. Totally recommend.

Its defnitly better then Aimlabs even though it looks really janky. Best way to use the trainer seems to be through 3rd party stat trackers. Once you get used to it you will never want to go back to Aimlabs. Took me two refunds to finally love Kovaak's but its defnitly worth the patience.
idk if this game is good i just kinda click balls and aim at pills while watching other people do it on twitch :) good game would recommend
Best aim trainer there is. Feels very smooth and has a lot of customization options.
Good aim practice software for competitive gaming. Playlists are really helpful and enjoyable.

practice 15-30 mins a day and everytime before you get ready to play a game and you will evenutally have ungodly aim and get accused of cheating even though you arent. it will feel good. you wont notice the improvement as most wont but your opponents will.

Hey, the KovaaK is essentially a gym subscription for your aim because it is pure work, and it is boring without the drive to push yourself. It lacks the cool lore or aesthetics but if you are looking for an app to improve your aim in Apex or Valorant, then there is nothing better than this. With over 25,000 community workouts, you get everything from improving micromovements to learning how to track moving targets using precise physics. This app costs some money, and will not give you any game sense but if you are struggling in hard-stuck ranks and need crack mouse aim, then it is a must buy.
very good game would reccomend helps a lot with aim and is fun

Definitely better than AimLabs and its not even close. AimLabs is clunky and the UI is disgusting. Plus if you want to use it to its highest capabilities, YOU STILL HAVE TO PAY FOR IT. Kovaaks has been and always will be the best aim trainer out there.
great game, but please remove the dust/explosion every time I kill a target. its very annoying and distracting

For whatever reason this game doesn't register mouse clicks, like way too often. So many times I have to click on a target twice, thrice, sometimes 5 or 6 times before I "hit" it. For an aim trainer that costs money it just isn't worth imo.
really worth it tbh never expected it to be different than aimlabs which is free but it really has alot of variations and better tasks with such details, quite worth the buy

Better than Aimlabs, Aimlabs locks a ton of ♥♥♥♥ behind a subscription but the subscription is ass might as well just spend a small amount on a game that you don't have to spend more money on later. Also, menus in Kovaak's are a billion times better than the cluttered slop of Aimlabs. Highly recommend this over Aimlabs ALSO they have a playlist for The Finals ands its a really good one so if you play The Finals get Kovaak's.

Game keeps changing my resolution, then freezing, then resetting the current practice, then changing the resolution again. The game worked very well for the 2 hour refund period and now it sucks. Do not buy, just use aimlabs.

Edit. The app stopped working and I originally didn't understand why. After a little research it seems Kovaak's cannot handle 8k polling or cpu's struggle with Kovaak's at that polling rate. Either way, Ideally I'd practice with the same settings i play with which is not possible. Since I upgraded my mouse after I purchased this, it is what it is... but again its just not ideal.
A genuinely awful game made popular because it's only competitor is even worse.

TLDR:I spend more time in kovaaks struggling with its systems performance and ui then I do aim training.

I tried aim labs and didn't like the subscription model so I tried kovaaks.  kovaaks may have everything you need but the ui is bad. the performance is terrible with no good reason for it to struggle the way it does.  

the playlists are pretty badly curated.  with huge jumps in difficulty.  it doesn't do anything to make sense of the information it spits out.  it makes no suggestions for sensitivity or improving.  so while it is an aim trainer in the sense it has excersises.  it won't help you get better unless you engage with a larger community.  this is just an unneccessary difficulty that prevents you spending time on aim training. 

the ui colors and visuals are bad.  unpleasant even.

the ui is also extremely disorganized with names that don't do much to convey their meanings to you.  
playlists give very little information on what they contain as well.  

basically if you want to use kovaaks you NEED someone who has already set it up before to do it for you or teach you.

Ah yes, the gay version of Aimlabs. I really like this one. Aimlabs crashes everytime I try opening it so its nice to be able to finally aimtrain on something that will run on a potato Pc. I love the amount of scenarios it offers and imo its very beginner friendly.
have carpal tunnel and my aim still blows. 10/10 would recommend
Amazing aim trainer, really everything you could ever want. It's so relaxing, like meditation in a way:D
i bought this specifically to ragebait battledads and uncs, accidentally improved my aim so much i get called a cheater in the process. 10/10, i want to suck off whoever made this
relaxing game + listen to music and chill
I got it and almost instantly saw an improvement in performance and consistency. I highly recommend if you want to push yourself to the next level
Good and cool if you want to improve aim and mouse control. The dopamine hit when u get a new PB is so good its even better than nutting.
helped my aim for multipul fps games way better than aimlabs personally highly Recommend.

Even though i have so little time on it, this has changed how i have aimed for the better.
I am already improving with just 7 hours on this. I highly recommend getting this if you suck at fps.
after finding out how to actually practice for ingame improvement i hit immortal in 2 weeks and now im addicted and cant quit
You charge me ten dollars and beg me for more money on the home screen yet the ♥♥♥♥♥♥♥ crosshair doesn't work properly this ♥♥♥♥ is ass don't buy it.
Good aim trainer and surprisingly has much better and smoother performance than Aimlabs
good game, some of the scenarios are laggy, that is my only gripe
Aim trainers dont work. Please just quit gaming if u struggle to aim; its down to pure genetics.

i think this is the objectively best aim trainer, i had 88 hours on Aimlabs and i didnt wanna switch that much, but once you switch you realise how much easier and more accesable everything feels, so much more tasks and easier to find aswell, aimlabs still have the better benchmarks imo, but this aim trainer feels so nice. if you spend a lot of time on aimtrainers, this should be your obvious choice

First time getting into kovaaks. I been with aiming.pro for years since I'm a macOS player its harder to find different games and aim trainers that are compatible with Mac software. One thing I've always did was change my Sens which was beginner mistake which messed up my settings. I would say stick to the scenarios your good at. Mainly focus on tracking, target switching and click timing, work on micro corrections and make a daily aim routine 10-15min. 1 tip I would say is find your Sens first, then stick to that Sens. Also have a good setup, I got a low end setup so i'm not that good. ALSO BIG WARNING DO NOT GET A APPLE MONITOR. uhh thank you for reading. Shout me out pls :D ( YT is Kabuto21godV2 much appreciated if you sub :). ty again

addicting asf seing some number go up all the time feels so good but its also you against you which can be frustrating especialy when you have been trying to break ur record for 3 hours straight and ur 2 points away so you throw ur mouse against the wall but overall good cop would buy again
♥♥♥♥♥♥♥ devs pick and choose which highscores to remove, biggest waste of 10 bucks youll ever spend
good aimtrainer known as the best alot of options to practice could be lighter on graphic weight but work fine with corrent rtx s cards
